subtitle: Mohenjo-daro Speaks in Clay, Bronze and Stone

panels.action: Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro stands amid half-buried walls and a twilight sky, holding a small clay figurine in one hand and a gleaming bronze in the other, the ghostly city stretching behind like a silent maze.

dialogue.text: In the stillness of Mohenjo-daro, Harappan hands shaped bronze, stone and clay into faces that outlived their names, and a lost city spoke through a few enduring bodies.

i18n.hi: मोहेंजोदाड़ो की ख़ामोशी में, हरप्पा के हाथों ने काँसे, पत्थर और मिट्टी से ऐसे चेहरे गढ़े जो नामों से भी ज़्यादा जीए, और डूबी हुई बस्ती कुछ बची हुई देहों से बोल उठी।

panels.action: From high above, the baked-brick city of Mohenjo-daro spreads out near the Indus, straight streets, wells and courtyards alive with tiny figures, while Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro walks along a lane carrying a basket of clay.

dialogue.text: Around 2500 BCE, where the Indus wandered and the dust shone like copper, Mohenjo-daro rose in brick and order, a city planned like a thought held firmly in the mind.

i18n.hi: करीब २५०० ईसा पूर्व, जहाँ सिंधु मँडराती थी और धूल ताँबे सी चमकती थी, वहाँ ईंटों और क़ायदे से बसता गया मोहेंजोदाड़ो — जैसे किसी मन में साफ़-साफ़ सोचा हुआ ख़याल।

panels.action: Inside a modest workshop, Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro kneels on a reed mat surrounded by terracotta figurines, clay lumps, stone tools and tiny steatite blocks while sunlight slices in through a high window.

dialogue.text: Here, in a quiet room off a crowded lane, hands rubbed clay, ground stone and weighed metal. They made objects for play, for trade, for status, perhaps for prayer—and for eyes now long turned to ash.

i18n.hi: यहीं, चहल-पहल वाली गली से लगी एक शांत कोठरी में, हाथ मिट्टी मलते, पत्थर घिसते, धातु तौलते। खिलौने, सौदे का सामान, रुतबे की चीज़ें, शायद पूजा की भी — उन आँखों के लिए, जो अब बस राख हैं।

panels.action: Close on the artist’s weathered hands pressing soft clay into a small female figurine, pinching out hips and breasts, adding a fan-shaped headdress and necklace with quick, practiced movements.

dialogue.text: Terracotta figures grew from his fingers—broad hips, heavy ornaments, elaborate headdresses. Were they goddesses, mothers, dolls, or all of these? The clay did not answer. It only kept the shape.

i18n.hi: उसकी उँगलियों से टेराकोटा पुतलियाँ निकलीं — भारी नितंब, मोटे गहने, टेढ़े-मेढ़े मुकुट। देवी थीं, माँएँ, गुड़िया, या सब कुछ एक साथ? मिट्टी ने कुछ न कहा, बस आकार संभाले रही।

panels.action: In a corner of the workshop, Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro crouches by a small clay furnace, placing wax-coated clay cores and bits of copper into crucibles, smoke drifting upward.

dialogue.text: Metal too obeyed. Wax forms were wrapped in clay, copper heated with care. The lost-wax casting took patience, knowledge of heat, of air, of when the glow was ready and when it would betray you.

i18n.hi: धातु भी मान जाती थी। मोम के रूपों पर मिट्टी लिपटी, ताँबा सँभालकर गर्म हुआ। लॉस्ट-वैक्स ढलाई में धैर्य चाहिए था, आँच की समझ, हवा की पहचान, और उस पल की नब्ज़ जब चमक साथ दे, न दगा।

panels.action: The artist breaks open a cooled clay mould, revealing a slender bronze female figure still dull, as dust falls away in the shaft of light cutting through the dim room.

dialogue.text: From the cracked shell of clay, a body of bronze emerged—bare, slim, bracelets climbing her arm, hand on hip, chin lifted. Only 10.8 centimetres high, yet standing as if the street was hers.

i18n.hi: फूटी हुई मिट्टी के खोल से काँसे की देह निकली — नग्न, पतली, कलाई से कंधे तक चूड़ियाँ, कमर पर हाथ, ठुड्डी तनी हुई। बस १०.८ सेंटीमीटर, पर खड़ी यूँ जैसे पूरी गली उसकी हो।

panels.shot: slow push-in on figurine and artist
panels.action: Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro studies the tiny bronze girl lying in his palm, her posture confident, bangles etched, hair coiled, while other unfinished figurines blur in the background.

dialogue.speaker: harappan_artists
dialogue.text: You will not speak my name, little one. But perhaps you will outlive it.

i18n.hi: "तू मेरा नाम नहीं बोलेगा, नन्ही। पर शायद मुझसे ज़्यादा जीएगा।"

panels.action: The workshop scene fades into an excavation trench millennia later: the same corner of earth, now cut open, with archaeologists’ tools around as Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro appears like a transparent ghost, watching.

dialogue.text: Centuries turned, the furnaces cooled forever. The city sank, brick by brick, into silt and silence. When men returned with spades and notebooks, they did not find his name. They found her.

i18n.hi: सदियाँ पलटीं, भट्ठियाँ हमेशा को ठंडी पड़ीं। शहर ईंट-दर-ईंट गारे और सन्नाटे में धँस गया। जब लोग कुदाल और कापी लेकर लौटे, उन्हें उसका नाम नहीं मिला — वो मिली।

panels.action: A modern gloved hand gently lifts the tiny bronze girl from dusty soil while, faintly above, the ghostly Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro looks on from the trench edge, expression calm and unreadable.

dialogue.text: In a house of Mohenjo-daro, excavators uncovered the small bronze. They saw her stance, her armful of bangles, and, moved by her poise, called her a dancing girl, though no dance was written in the ground.

i18n.hi: मोहेंजोदाड़ो के एक घर में खुदाई से यह छोटी काँसी निकली। उन्होंने उसकी अदा देखी, बाँह भर चूड़ियाँ देखीं, और उसकी ठसक से असर खाकर उसे नाचने वाली लड़की कहा, जबकि धरती पर कहीं कोई नाच लिखा न था।

panels.action: The bronze girl now stands in a glass case under bright lights, visitors’ silhouettes around her; in the reflection of the glass, Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro’s face appears faintly, watching as captions are added nearby.

dialogue.text: The label gave her a trade and a story she had never spoken. Perhaps she danced. Perhaps she walked the lanes as a child of some household. The truth stayed folded inside the bronze.

i18n.hi: उस नाम ने उसे एक धंधा दे दिया, एक कहानी जो उसने कभी नहीं कही। शायद वह नाचती थी। शायद किसी घर की बच्ची बनकर गलियाँ नापती थी। सच्चाई काँसे के भीतर मोड़ी ही रही।

panels.action: Back in the Bronze Age workshop, Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro now sits with a small steatite block in a clamp, carefully carving the beginnings of a human chest and beard with a fine tool.

dialogue.text: Stone demanded a different patience. Soft steatite yielded slowly, line by line, to the tool. From it he raised not a full body, but a bust, as if the lower half had never been needed.

i18n.hi: पत्थर अलग सब्र माँगता था। नरम स्टिएटाइट औज़ार के नीचे रेखा-रेखा घिसकर झुकता। उसने पूरा जिस्म नहीं, सिर्फ़ एक उरोज़ तराशा — जैसे नीचे का हिस्सा कभी ज़रूरी ही न रहा हो।

panels.shot: close-up three-quarter view of emerging bust
panels.action: The partly finished bust sits in the artist's hands: a bearded face with calm eyes, fillet across the forehead, cloak patterned with trefoil designs over one shoulder, the lower body sketched but not yet carved.

dialogue.text: A heavy-lidded gaze, close-cropped beard, a band around the head, trefoil patterns he once saw on cloth cut into stone. Was this a leader, a ritual specialist, a rich elder, or a type used again and again?

i18n.hi: भारी पलकें, करीने से कटी दाढ़ी, सिर पर पट्टी, कपड़े पर देखे तिरमुड़े फूल अब पत्थर में उकेरे। क्या ये सरदार था, रीत-रस्मों का जानकार, कोई अमीर बूढ़ा, या एक साँचा, जिसे बार-बार दोहराया गया?

panels.shot: intimate low-angle shot of artist and bust
panels.action: Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro holds the steatite bust up toward the light from a high window, dust motes floating around, as if testing how shadow changes the stern face.

dialogue.speaker: harappan_artists
dialogue.text: You will watch long after our streets crumble. But you will watch in silence.

i18n.hi: "तुम बहुत देर तक देखोगे, हमारी गलियाँ मिट जाने के बाद भी। पर देखोगे चुपचाप।"

panels.action: Millennia later, an archaeologist’s trowel reveals the steatite bust in a room at Mohenjo-daro; the bust lies half embedded in soil while, just behind, the faint outline of Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro kneels, mirroring the pose.

dialogue.text: When this face of stone surfaced in the 1920s, its calm authority struck the excavators. They named it a Priest-King. Yet they had no throne, no inscription, no clear sign of temple or crown.

i18n.hi: जब यह पत्थर का चेहरा १९२० के दशक में मिला, उसकी ठंडी शांति ने खुदाई करने वालों को चौंका दिया। उन्होंने इसे "पुरोहित-राजा" कह दिया, जबकि न सिंहासन मिला, न लेख, न मंदिर या मुकुट की पक्की निशानी।

panels.action: The steatite bust now rests in a secure display case, light raking across its features; beyond, visitors gesture and talk, while Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro’s reflection overlaps the stern carved face.

dialogue.text: The name clung to him, as if certainty could be carved as easily as trefoils. Perhaps he was important. Perhaps he was one portrait among many, now lost. The stone remained, the title floated above it like mist.

i18n.hi: नाम उससे चिपक गया, जैसे यक़ीन भी तिरमुड़े फूलों की तरह आसानी से तराशा जा सकता हो। हो सकता है वो बड़ा आदमी हो। हो सकता है कई चेहरों में बस एक हो, बाकी गुम। पत्थर बचा रहा, ख़िताब धुएँ-सा ऊपर तैरता रहा।

panels.shot: tabletop wide shot of varied artefacts
panels.action: Spread across a low table are terracotta female figurines, animal toys on wheels, shell bangles, a small copper spearhead and beads, with Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro reaching in to adjust a figurine lovingly.

dialogue.text: His city’s art was not only bronze and steatite. There were clay women and clay carts, humped bulls, tiny toys that once rolled on packed earth, shells shaped into bangles that rang softly when arms moved through the crowd.

i18n.hi: उसके शहर की कला सिर्फ़ काँसा और स्टिएटाइट नहीं थी। मिट्टी की औरतें, मिट्टी की गाड़ियाँ, कूबड़ वाले बैल, नन्हे खिलौने जो कभी सख़्त ज़मीन पर लुढ़कते थे, सीप से बनी चूड़ियाँ जो भीड़ में चलते हाथों पर हल्के से छनकती होंगी।

panels.shot: close-up on the artist threading a seal
panels.action: Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro carefully engraves a tiny steatite seal showing a seated, horned figure surrounded by animals, Indus signs above, then dips it into a greenish liquid before threading it on a cord.

dialogue.text: On seals, animals lined up with care. A horned figure seated cross-legged with beasts around him was carved on one small stone. Later eyes would see Pashupati, even a proto-Shiva, but the seal itself gave no certain claim.

i18n.hi: मोहरों पर जानवर सलीके से कतार में। एक सींगदार आकृति, जानवरों से घिरी, पद्मासन में, एक छोटे पत्थर पर उकेरी गई। बाद की नज़रों ने उसमें पशुपति देखा, यहाँ तक कि आदि-शिव, मगर खुद मोहर ने कोई पक्की पहचान नहीं दी।

panels.action: In a modern lab, the same seal lies under a magnifying lens beside careful drawings and notes, while the translucent figure of Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro stands behind the scientist, looking down with quiet curiosity.

dialogue.text: Scholars traced each line, compared symbols, weighed possibilities. They spoke of trade, of ritual, of status and identity. They knew that readings were provisional, that the seal script still held its tongue.

i18n.hi: विद्वान हर रेखा पर चले, चिह्नों की तुलना की, संभावना तौली। वे व्यापार, रीत, रुतबा और पहचान की बात करते रहे। उन्हें मालूम था ये पढ़तें अस्थायी हैं, कि मोहर की लिपि अब भी ज़ुबान बंद किए बैठे है।

panels.shot: intimate close-up on terracotta figurine and bangle
panels.action: A terracotta female figurine with detailed ornaments lies beside a modern photograph of bangles from Harappan levels; ghostly hands of Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro seem to hover as if placing the bangle on a living arm.

dialogue.text: New studies looked again at bangles, at figurine bodies and ornaments. They asked if these clay women showed goddesses, fashion, ideals of the body, or daily life. The answers shifted with each careful comparison.

i18n.hi: नई पढ़ाइयों ने फिर से चूड़ियों, पुतली के जिस्म और गहनों को टटोला। सवाल उठे — क्या ये मिट्टी की औरतें देवी दिखाती हैं, फैशन, शरीर का आदर्श, या रोज़मर्रा की ज़िंदगी? हर ठोस तुलना के साथ जवाब खिसकते गए।

panels.shot: wide shot blending ancient street with museum gallery
panels.action: Half the panel shows a bustling Harappan street with people wearing ornaments, handling seals and toys; the other half dissolves into a modern gallery with the same objects in cases, Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro standing at the boundary.

dialogue.text: Between their world and ours, words grow quickly. Dancer, priest, king, proto-god. These names can delight, but they can also harden, turning open questions into neat tales the earth never promised.

i18n.hi: उनकी दुनिया और हमारी दुनिया के बीच, शब्द तेज़ी से उगते हैं। नर्तकी, पुरोहित, राजा, आदि-देव। ये नाम लुभाते हैं, पर सख़्त भी हो जाते हैं — खुले सवालों को साफ़-सुथरी कहानियों में बदल देते हैं, जो मिट्टी ने कभी वादा ही नहीं कीं।

panels.shot: slow push-in on artist’s contemplative face
panels.action: Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro stands alone amid shadowy outlines of the bronze girl, the stone bust, seals and figurines floating around him like memories, his face calm yet searching.

dialogue.speaker: harappan_artists
dialogue.text: Look at them with care. Let wonder be wide enough to hold doubt.

i18n.hi: उन्हें गौर से देखो। हैरत को इतना फैला रहने दो कि उसमें शक भी समा सके।

panels.action: At sunset over the ruins of Mohenjo-daro, the Great Bath and brick mounds glow amber; in the foreground in silhouette stand the tiny bronze girl and stone bust on a plinth, with Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro’s faint figure behind them.

dialogue.text: From a face in stone and a body in bronze, we learn not only Indus skill, but humility: that true wonder grows stronger when it tells the truth and lets the ancient silence speak beside our stories.

i18n.hi: पत्थर के चेहरे और काँसे की देह से हमें सिर्फ़ सिंधु की हुनरमंदी नहीं, विनम्रता भी मिलती है — कि असली विस्मय सच बोलकर और पुरानी ख़ामोशी को हमारी कहानियों के साथ-जगह देकर ही और मज़बूत होता है।

title_i18n.en: A Face in Stone a Body in Bronze
title_i18n.hi: पत्थर का चेहरा, काँसे का जिस्म

subtitle_i18n.en: Mohenjo-daro Speaks in Clay, Bronze and Stone
subtitle_i18n.hi: मोहेंजोदाड़ो की मिट्टी, काँसा और पत्थर की ज़ुबान
